newsSince: No material company-specific news inside the June 9-23 window. Q3 FY26 results (EPS $0.77 vs $0.67 expected; sales +22%) and a raised FY26 outlook (sales $2.33-2.35B, adj. EPS $3.75-4.00) were reported May 6, pre-window. Surging tungsten pricing is boosting revenue/EPS but pressuring working capital and free cash flow; no new June data point.

sources.label: Kennametal lifts Q3 FY26, raises outlook (StockTitan)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent under-covered AI supply-chain lens: May 6 beat and raised FY guidance (aero/defense/energy tied to AI power generation) pre-date the window but keep the carbide-tooling/reshoring case intact. No new June catalyst; small positive carry on momentum still recent -> hold.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Raised FY26 guidance; aero/defense/AI-power-generation demand and pricing/share gains
verdicts.primaryRisk: Tungsten-price volatility pressuring working capital and free cash flow
verdicts.bull: Carbide tooling/wear materials enable reshoring of precision thermal/mechanical parts; pricing and defense orders lift margins.
verdicts.neutral: On-thesis latent industrial name with a guidance raise, but the catalyst predates the window and tungsten swings cloud cash flow.
verdicts.bear: Tungsten-driven revenue gains are partly inflationary and crimp FCF (~-30% of adj. net income); demand is cyclical.
